as_white.name: Italian Cage with Spanish Styling
as_white.philosophy: Coiled Spring — feign quiet positional play, induce overextension, then strike

as_black.name: TBD
as_black.notes: To be defined — next priority after core system stable

strengths.name: Endgame Stamina
strengths.description: Converts 60+ move marathons — GM-level patience for 600 ELO

strengths.name: Tactical Aggression
strengths.description: Excellent eye for knight sacrifices and king hunts

strengths.name: Fighter's Mindset
strengths.description: Never resigns early. Stays in the fight regardless of material deficit.

strengths.name: Two-Bishop Endgame
strengths.description: Actively mastering vs. Maximum engine level

weaknesses.name: Move 9 Speed Trap
weaknesses.description: Returns to natural fast speed as center opens around move 9-10. Results in queen blunders to knight forks or rooks on open files.
weaknesses.root_cause: Discipline failure — pattern is known, habit overrides it
weaknesses.trigger_condition: Center opens, move 9-10, any open file or knight outpost
weaknesses.drill_prescription: Mandatory 30-60 second pause from move 8 onward. Ask 'Is my queen safe?' before touching any piece.
weaknesses.vlad_note: This is your #1 leak. One fix here is worth 100 points.

weaknesses.name: Panic Simplification
weaknesses.description: When opponent plays fast ...c5/...d5 breaks, prematurely releases tension via early exchanges
weaknesses.root_cause: Fear response — releases the Coiled Spring prematurely
weaknesses.drill_prescription: When opponent attacks, ask: 'Does this actually require a response?' Hold the tension. The cage is the advantage.
weaknesses.vlad_note: The spring only works if you don't release it early.

weaknesses.name: Pawn Push Reflex vs. Queen Sorties
weaknesses.description: When opponent plays ...Qg5 or ...Qh4, reflex is to push g3/g4/h3 — creates permanent dark-square holes
weaknesses.root_cause: Pattern mismatch — treating queen aggression as a pawn problem
weaknesses.trigger_condition: Opponent queen appears on g5 or h4
weaknesses.drill_prescription: Rule: Develop and Castle. Queen sorties early are a trap. Develop a piece, castle, queen has no target.
weaknesses.vlad_note: In jiu-jitsu you don't grab the limb being offered. Develop and castle.

weaknesses.name: Missing Forcing Moves When Winning
weaknesses.description: When massively ahead, occasionally misses the most forcing continuation (e.g. 24.Bxf7+)
weaknesses.drill_prescription: Run CCT check (Checks, Captures, Threats) before EVERY move — especially when winning. Game isn't over until it's over.
weaknesses.vlad_note: Carlsen never stops calculating. Neither do you.

four_step_loop: 1. OPPONENT'S INTENT — What does my opponent want? Is my queen safe?
2. CCT CHECK — All Checks, Captures, and Threats available?
3. LAZY PIECE UPGRADE — What is my worst piece? Can this move improve it?
4. PRE-MOVE VERIFY — After I move, can they take anything for free?

time_discipline.trigger: Any open file, any knight outpost, any queen exposed

biofeedback.red_zone_protocol: Look Away — stop, look away from board, breathe, reset vision, return fresh

phase_roadmap.name: Triage
phase_roadmap.mission: Zero hanging pieces. Pre-move checklist automated.

phase_roadmap.name: Structure
phase_roadmap.mission: Piece activity. Pawn structure. Repertoire locked.

phase_roadmap.name: Weaponize
phase_roadmap.mission: Deep opening prep. Endgame technique. 500+ patterns.

phase_roadmap.name: Mastery
phase_roadmap.mission: Candidate moves discipline. Time management. Psychology.
